sentence: She is going to start a new job next month
hint: be + going to + base verb for plans
explanation: Going to for planned future: subject + be + going to + base verb.

sentence: I will call you when I arrive
hint: Will for promises and spontaneous decisions
explanation: 'Will' for promises. Note: after 'when', use present tense 'arrive', not future.

sentence: You should see a doctor about that cough
explanation: Modal 'should' + base form for advice: 'should see'.

sentence: They might come to the party tonight
explanation: 'Might' + base form for possibility: 'might come'.

sentence: We are meeting the clients at three tomorrow
explanation: Present continuous for scheduled future: 'are meeting'.

sentence: He must finish the report before Friday
explanation: 'Must' + base form for obligation: 'must finish'.

sentence: You should not eat so much sugar
explanation: 'Should not' for negative advice. 'Must' would be too strong here.

sentence: I am going to travel to Japan next summer
explanation: Going to for planned future: 'am going to travel'.

sentence: She can speak three languages fluently
explanation: 'Can' + base form for ability. Adverb 'fluently' at the end.

sentence: It will probably rain this afternoon
hint: Adverbs go between will and the main verb
explanation: 'Probably' goes between 'will' and the verb: 'will probably rain'.

sentence: You must not use your phone during the exam
explanation: 'Must not' for prohibition: 'must not use'.

sentence: They could arrive late because of the traffic
explanation: 'Could' + base form for possibility: 'could arrive'.

sentence: We will have finished the project by December
hint: Future perfect: will + have + past participle
explanation: Future perfect for completion before a deadline: 'will have finished'.

sentence: She might not be able to come tomorrow
explanation: 'Might not' for negative possibility. 'Be able to' replaces 'can' after modals.

sentence: You should have told me about the problem earlier
hint: Should have + past participle for past regret
explanation: 'Should have + past participle' for things that were advisable but didn't happen.

sentence: I will definitely be there on time
explanation: Adverb 'definitely' between 'will' and the verb: 'will definitely be'.

sentence: He is going to apply for the scholarship next week
explanation: Going to for intentions: 'is going to apply'.

sentence: We must leave now or we will miss the train
explanation: Two clauses: 'must leave now' (obligation) + 'will miss' (consequence).

sentence: She could have been a professional dancer
hint: Could have + past participle for unrealized past ability
explanation: 'Could have been' for something possible in the past that didn't happen.

sentence: They are not going to accept the offer
explanation: Negative going to: 'are not going to accept'.

sentence: You may use the computer after lunch
explanation: 'May' + base form for permission: 'may use'.

sentence: I will be working at the office all day tomorrow
explanation: Future continuous for ongoing future actions: 'will be working'.

sentence: He should not have spent all his money
explanation: 'Should not have + past participle' for past regret about something that happened.

sentence: We might go to the mountains this weekend
explanation: 'Might' for uncertain plans: 'might go'.

sentence: You will need to bring your passport to the airport
explanation: 'Will need to' for future necessity.
